core_principle.statement: Products are not standalone items; they are engineered components of an architectural system.
core_principle.interpretation: Every ceiling solution must be defined as part of a modular architectural engineering system.

product_system_philosophy.logic: Product → Module → System → Architectural Application → Execution
product_system_philosophy.anti_concept: Reject standalone product thinking without engineering context

product_definition_model.rule: No product exists without system classification

system_classification.name: Linear Ceiling Systems
system_classification.definition: Continuous engineered ceiling solutions based on linear modules

system_classification.name: Hybrid Architectural Systems
system_classification.definition: Combination of multiple ceiling system types

architecture_logic.system_flow: Architecture → Engineering Design → System Definition → Product Modules → Execution
architecture_logic.rule: Product cannot exist without system definition
architecture_logic.dependency: Every product must be traceable to an engineering system structure

engineering_constraints.modularity: All products must be modular system components
engineering_constraints.scalability: Must support architectural scaling without redesign
engineering_constraints.integration: Must integrate with other ceiling system modules
engineering_constraints.precision: Must follow AECS grid and geometry rules

product_identity_rules.naming_system: System-based naming only (no standalone product naming)

value_structure.core_value: Engineering reliability of architectural ceiling systems

market_positioning.category_shift: From ceiling manufacturer → Architectural Engineering System Provider
market_positioning.differentiation: We design systems, not sell products

user_perception: This is not a product catalog, this is a system portfolio
This company thinks in systems, not items

strategic_goal.objective: Eliminate product-level thinking and enforce system-level perception
strategic_goal.outcome: AvizSazeh is perceived as an architectural engineering system authority, not a manufacturer
